artifacts.summary: Implementation summary for fixing /tmp/ usage root cause across 26 files in two .opencode/ repositories

completion_data.completion_summary: Standardized all temp file references across 26 files in ~/.config/nvim/.opencode/ and ~/.dotfiles/.opencode/ to use the canonical specs/tmp/ pattern, eliminated bare and in-place .tmp suffix patterns, added mkdir -p specs/tmp guards, and added explicit /tmp/ prohibition to both AGENTS.md files.

memory_candidates.content: In LLM-driven agent systems (like OpenCode), documentation files ARE effectively code. The LLM reads skill files, command files, and example documents to learn patterns, then generates bash commands based on those patterns. Inconsistent temp file conventions in documentation (e.g., some files using specs/state.json.tmp, others using specs/tmp/state.json, others using state.json.tmp with no prefix) will cause the LLM to generate ad-hoc combinations that may include system /tmp/ paths. The fix is to: (1) make all examples consistent, (2) add explicit prohibitions in the primary context file (AGENTS.md) loaded at session start, and (3) add mkdir -p guards before all temp file writes.
